content.full_text:
State of California

Employment Training Panel

George Deukmejian
Governor

William Woods
Chairman

Elinor Glenn
Vice Chair

Arne Kalm
Member

Stephen Mack
Member

Rebecca H. Naman
Member

James Quillin
Member

Pat Williams
Member

James Brau
Interim Director

Rhonda E Johnson
Assistant Director

Peter G. DeMauro
General Counsel

General Office:
P.O. Box 826880, MIC 64
Sacramento, CA 94280-0001
(916) 323-5873

Regional Offices:
P.O. Box 826880, MIC 64
Sacramento, CA 94280-0001
(916) 323-1338

4640 Lankershim Blvd.,
Suite 311
No. Hollywood, CA 91602
(818) 505-6200

177 Bovet Road, Suite 180
San Mateo, CA 94402
(415) 573-3830

December 21, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Request for Written Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter requests the advice of the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding a conflict of interest disqualification and a particular vote of the Employment Training Panel.

THE FACTS

The Employment Training Panel is a state agency within the Employment Development Department consisting of seven appointed panel members. Two members of the Panel shall be appointed by the Speaker of the Assembly, two members of the Panel shall be appointed by the President pro Tempore of the Senate, and three members of the Panel shall be appointed by the Governor.1/

The Panel holds a public meeting monthly to consider funding for training programs as provided in its enabling legislation.2/ At its meeting of November 15/16, 1990, held in Fremont, California, the Panel considered a proposal from the United Auto Workers, Labor and Employment Training Corporation (UAW-LETC), to retrain certain workers at the Libbey-Owens-Ford Co. plant in Lathrop, California.

Panel Member Pat Williams is Vice President, Marketing, Research and Development for UAW-LETC. Under Government Code 87100, she disqualified herself from voting on this proposal due to a possible conflict of interest. At the time of the vote, three Panel Members

1/ Unemployment Insurance Code Section 10202.

2/ Unemployment Insurance Code Section 10200 et seq.

ETP's Rev. 5/90)

Scott Hallabrin                                    December 17, 1990
                                                            Page 2

were absent from the meeting, one due to travel outside the State of
California, the other two because of scheduled flight arrangements
from San Jose to Los Angeles.  This left four members present, with
Panel Member Williams disqualified from voting.

The Panel will appreciate the advice of the FPPC regarding the
following:

                            THE QUESTIONS

1.    Four members of the Panel is a quorum.  Can a Panel member
      with a conflict of interest participate under the "rule of
      necessity" to form a quorum of the Panel, but abstain from the
      vote without affecting the quorum?

2.    Was a quorum established without Panel Member Williams, since
      three Panel members were physically absent but not legally
      disqualified from voting?

3.    The vote on the issue was two " yes", one "no," one abstention
      (Panel Member Williams).  Did this constitute denial of the
      proposal?

In fairness to the applicant, the Chairman of the Panel suggested
that the Panel revisit the proposal at its December, 1990
meeting.   This was scheduled, and the full Panel voted to deny
funding of the proposal.

Please let me know if any additional information will be helpful in
your review of this situation.  We look forward to your reply.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Peter G. DeMauro
General Counsel


cc:   James M. Bratt, Executive Director
      William Woods, Chairman
